exemplar.title: Investigating the effect of temperature change on the lactase-catalyzed lactose hydrolysis

detailedExemplarData.title: Investigating the effect of temperature change on the lactase-catalyzed lactose hydrolysis

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Research question embedded in a specific biological and industrial context
Clear step-by-step protocol with volumes, temperatures and replicates
Key methodological considerations (controls, calibration, replicates) are identified and described
weaknesses: Some repetition in background detracts from concision
Justification for trial number and reaction time is missing
Critical methodological details (exact enzyme concentration after dilution, mixing method) are omitted
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student clearly situates the research question and provides a reproducible protocol, meriting performance in the 3â4 band for all strands. Minor omissions and uneven explanations prevent a full 5â6 rating, so a holistic score of 4 reflects the strong context and reproducibility balanced against gaps in justification and detail.

strengths: Recording and processing of raw and processed data is communicated clearly and with precision
Accurate data processing (means, SD, ANOVA, post-hoc) largely free of arithmetic error
weaknesses: Uncertainty propagation is not carried through to processed data or graphs
Some formatting inconsistencies (figure numbering, unit notation) remain
Statistical assumptions and effect sizes are not discussed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands demonstrate topâband performance, but significant omissions in uncertainty propagation and formatting issues place the student solidly in the 3â4 band overall. A score of 4 acknowledges the highâquality data treatment alongside notable gaps in uncertainty and presentation.

strengths: Conclusion is fully consistent with analysed data and explicitly justified by means, SD and statistical significance
Relevant comparison with peerâreviewed studies reinforces and contextualises findings
weaknesses: Conclusion paragraph ends abruptly without full closure
Could more fully discuss substrate matrix effects on glucometer readings
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands reach the 5â6 band: the conclusion is wellâjustified by the analysis and placed in scientific context. Minor issues of concision and completeness prevent a topâband 6, but the holistic quality warrants a score of 5.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses are identified and their relative impacts are explained
Realistic improvements are linked to the identified limitations and their effects on accuracy
weaknesses: Some improvement suggestions could more precisely quantify expected gains
Discussion of error magnitude could be more detailed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The explanation of specific weaknesses and their impacts demonstrates depth and critical insight consistent with the 5â6 band. While some improvement proposals lack quantitative detail, the overall sophistication of the evaluation justifies a holistic score of 5.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student states that optimum hydrolysis occurs at 40 Â°C and supports this with mean values, ANOVA significance and non-overlapping error bars, explicitly linking to denaturation above 40 Â°C. The conclusion is **fully consistent with the analysed data**; while articulation could be more concise, the justification meets the 5â6 standard, earning 5.

Raw-data tables list all trials with units and uncertainty (Â±1 mg dLâ»Â¹), processed tables show percentage change, means and SD, and graphs/error bars are referenced. Layout is **clear and internally consistent**, though figure numbering is slightly disordered. Both clarity and precision are demonstrated, meeting the 5â6 descriptor; a small formatting lapse justifies a 5 rather than 6.

Data processing includes percentage change calculations, means, SD, one-way ANOVA and Tukey post-hoc test, all correctly applied to address the research question. Sample SD calculation is shown. Values in processed table match raw data with no noticeable arithmetic errors. This constitutes **appropriate and accurate processing**, satisfying the top band; minor rounding inconsistencies justify a 5 rather than 6.

Three **specific weaknesses** (wide temp intervals, time lag cooling, blood glucometer use) are identified and their effects on accuracy or validity are explained, demonstrating understanding of relative impact (e.g., mis-measurement of actual reaction temp, matrix interference). The depth of explanation places the work in the 5â6 band; slight brevity on quantitative size of error warrants a 5.

Two peer-reviewed studies are discussed, both reporting optimal temperatures near 38â40 Â°C, and the student explicitly explains how this corroborates their findings and increases reliability. This constitutes a **relevant, justified comparison to accepted scientific context**, meeting the criteria for the 5â6 band; 5 is appropriate given brief rather than exhaustive discussion.

The student embeds the research question in a **clear, specific biological and industrial context**, linking optimum lactase temperature to the manufacture of lactose-free milk for lactose-intolerant consumers. Background paragraphs outline the biochemical reaction, public-health need and previous literature, so the reader understands *why* the question matters and how it fits existing knowledge. This exceeds a broad outline and merits the 5â6 band; some minor repetition prevents full sophistication, so 5 is appropriate.

Uncertainties for temperature (Â±1 Â°C) and glucose (Â±1 mg dLâ»Â¹) are indicated, and SD is calculated, but **instrument resolution/percent error is not propagated** to processed data or graph, nor are error bars linked to measurement uncertainty versus variability. Thus consideration is present yet contains significant omissions, aligning with the middle band (3â4); a 3 is appropriate given the limited depth.

Improvements are **realistic and directly linked** to each weakness (narrower temperature range, continuous temperature monitoring, alternative quantitative glucose assays). Some are explained (e.g., GOx method increases accuracy) but others remain generic or conflated with extensions rather than concrete procedural changes. This fulfills the description level (3â4) with reasonable depth, so a 4 is assigned.

Key methodological considerations are **identified and described** (control of milk type, enzyme amount, time, calibration of glucometer, six replicates, temperature range justified via preliminary test). However, explanations of *why* each measure secures relevant and sufficient data are uneven; e.g. justification for six trials or 10-min reaction period is not provided and the appropriateness of a blood glucometer for milk is only briefly acknowledged. These omissions keep the strand in the described, not fully explained, level (3â4 band), so 4 is awarded.

The step-by-step protocol includes volumes, temperatures, numbers of trials and timing, allowing another investigator to reproduce the work with **few ambiguities**. Nevertheless, some essential details are missing or unclear (exact concentration of enzyme after dilution, mixing method, precise timing referenceâ10 min vs 20 min stated in question header, whether samples remain in bath during reaction). Because these gaps could cause minor confusion but replication remains feasible, the methodology sits comfortably at 3â4 band; 4 reflects the upper end of that description band.

annotations.comment: Preliminary tests truncated at 60 Â°C are described but raw data are omitted. Suggest including a summary table or graph of the 60â80 Â°C trials to justify the final temperature range selection.

The research question is embedded within a specific biological and industrial context, clearly linking optimal lactase temperature to lactoseâfree milk production. This establishes strong relevance and demonstrates a mature understanding of why the investigation matters.

Background paragraphs repeat key points about lactose intolerance, which detracts from concision. Consider consolidating overlapping sentences to avoid redundancy and enhance focus on critical context.

The background relies heavily on general definitions without critiquing source reliability. Suggest adding brief commentary on why Reactome (2021) or NIH (2018) were selected and their suitability for this context.

The null hypothesis states âno significant differenceâ but doesnât define significance criteria. Recommend specifying the statistical test (e.g., ANOVA) and alpha level here to make the hypothesis operational.

Table of variables suggests kinetic energy âmight decreaseâ at high temperature. This mischaracterizes molecular kinetics; kinetic energy increases with temperature. Clarify that denaturation, not decreased energy, reduces rate.
The protocol should specify the final enzyme concentration after dilution (UI/mL) and mixing method to ensure reproducibility. Include exact volumes and mixing duration.
List items include residual LaTeX/math symbols (e.g., â\overline{a}â). Remove or convert these to standard text to prevent reader confusion.

Stating âno ethical considerationsâ overlooks waste disposal and biohazard risks. Recommend adding measures for safe disposal of milk and enzyme solutions to address environmental and ethical aspects.
Step âmake 30 lactase enzyme solutionsâ conflicts with earlier six preparations. Clarify the total number of solutions needed for reproducibility and avoid ambiguity on reagent use.
Qualitative observation is limited to color and texture. Recommend noting any changes in viscosity, pH or precipitation to deepen insight into temperature effects.
Raw data table clearly shows initial/final values, uncertainties (Â±1 mg/dL) and replicates. Presentation is precise and supports transparent data recording.
Percentage change table uses â(0.001)%â as a unit, which is nonstandard and confusing. Replace with a clear â%â label and verify all unit notations for consistency.
The narrative references a trendline but does not label axes or include RÂ². Add axis titles with units and report the coefficient of determination to strengthen data presentation.
Figure numbering is inconsistent (prediction sketch labeled âFigure 2â). Ensure all figures are sequentially numbered and correctly referenced in the text.
Sample standard deviation formula squares raw values instead of deviations from the mean. Recompute using $(x_i - \overline{x})^2$ to obtain correct dispersion metrics.
Statistical analysis omits mention of ANOVA assumptions (normality, homoscedasticity). Discuss how these were assessed or justify omission to reinforce test validity.
The report cites a small p-value but lacks effect size (e.g., etaâsquared) to quantify practical significance. Include an effect size to complement statistical findings.
Tukey post hoc results are shown but narrative does not explicitly state which pairs exceed Q critical. Recommend annotating significant comparisons directly in the table or text.
The conclusion paragraph ends abruptly midâsentence and omits explicit closure. Revise for completeness and ensure each claim about enzyme activity is fully articulated.
Comparison with Popescu et al. (2021) is well integrated and justifies findings within accepted literature, enhancing the credibility of the conclusion.
Evaluation identifies specific methodological weaknesses (temperature intervals, time lag, glucometer bias) and explains their impact on accuracy, demonstrating critical insight.
Some improvement suggestions remain generic (e.g., âwider temperature rangeâ) without quantifying expected accuracy gains. Tie each proposal to the identified error magnitude where possible.
Reference entries lack consistent formatting (missing DOIs, inconsistent URLs). Adopt a single citation style (e.g., APA) to improve professionalism and traceability.
Bibliography intermixes inâtext citation elements (access dates within entry). Separate in-text citations from reference list and standardize layout.
Processed data presentation omits propagation of measurement uncertainties into percentage change. Include uncertainty bars calculated from both instrument error and sample variability.
Trendline discussion omits RÂ². Report the coefficient of determination to quantify how well the model fits the processed data.
Variable definitions in the standard deviation section mislabel Î¼ as population mean; define \overline{x} correctly as the sample mean to avoid statistical confusion.
ANOVA table shows F, F critical but narrative omits explicit comparison. State âF (4,25) = 162.75 > Fâcritâ = 2.76, p < 0.05â to justify rejecting the null hypothesis.
Discussion compares different substrates but does not address potential matrix effects (blood vs. milk). Consider elaborating on how substrate differences might influence glucometer readings.
